reasoning:
The rate of ACLED 'Strategic developments' in Benin needs to exceed 8.08 (i.e., >= 9 events in a 30-day window). An average of 8.08 events per 30 days over a 12-month period implies a total of 97 events. Historically, if events follow a constant Poisson distribution of rate 8.08, the chance of observing 9 or more events is ~42%. However, evidence strongly points to a sharply escalating conflict in Benin: jihadist expansion from the Sahel has turned the border regions into active combat zones in early 2025, and Q1 2025 military deaths have already exceeded all of 2024. Furthermore, domestic political tensions are starting to rise ahead of the 2026 elections following the thwarting of a coup last year. Because 2024/2025 exhibits an ongoing escalation, recent and upcoming 30-day windows will very likely possess higher rates than the lagging 12-month average.

For the nearest target (June 29, 2025), a substantial portion of the 30-day window has already occurred during a documented period of heightened military and jihadist deployments, so likelihood of exceeding 8 events is very high (lambda likely >11).
As we project further into late 2025, this rate might plateau, revert closer to the mean, or be disrupted by political developments, so uncertainty increases and probabilities move closer to 50%.
